lyrics:
Name's Bentley
Most call me Bent
Worked here since the floor was poured
They used to say we were a family
Then a team
Then just... The Bent
Guess that stuck

Thirty-six years on this stretch of floor
I've fixed this gate, this lock, that door
I watched them come. I watched them go
I swept the ruins of what we'd known
They called it progress. They changed the sign
Said "We bend the future."-well, so did I

We hit the targets. We met the mark
They lit the fuse and praised the spark
They took their cut. We bore the cost
And now I'm left to weigh what's lost
I ask aloud, to ghost and frost-
We played the game... but at what cost

I knew their names, before the scans
Before the charts replaced our hands
We hung our coats on numbered racks
We cheered when Mary's boy came back
And every year, the list grew thin
They paved the lot and painted "WIN"

We hit the targets. We met the mark
They lit the fuse and praised the spark
They took their cut. We bore the cost
And now I'm left to weigh what's lost
I ask aloud, to ghost and frost-
We played the game... but at what cost

They got their growth
They rang that bell
They sold their lies
And still slept well
I locked the gates
The wind blew through
And I remember what they won't do

We bend, we broke, we gave, we stayed
We traded time the rich repaid
They walked away when lines were crossed
And left us holding all that's lost
So I sign off. I drop the key
The lights go dark behind me
The Bent remember what they lost-
They say it's paid
But at what cost

At what cost
At what cost
persona:
## 4Castle

The Future of Rock, The Power of Harmony

**4Castle **is a genre-defying, five-piece supergroup under Maricopa Records, built on a foundation of powerful vocal harmonies, intricate musicianship, and fearless innovation. Rooted in rock, but unbound by limitations, their music fuses elements of progressive rock, funk, Latin rhythms, blues, and folk, delivering a sound that is both technically complex and emotionally resonant.

### **A Band Like No Other**

Each member of **4Castle **brings a distinct voice-both musically and vocally-to the mix:
🎸 **Raven Cortez**(Lead Guitar, Vocals) – A fiery virtuoso with Latin and blues influences, weaving face-melting solos into every performance.
🎸 **Skylar “Sky” Monroe**(Rhythm Guitar, Vocals) – The poetic soul of the band, crafting melodic foundations and lyrically rich storytelling.
🎹 **Jonah Vega**(Keyboards, Vocals) – The sonic architect, layering psychedelic synths, Hammond organ swells, and cinematic orchestrations.
🎸 **Malcolm “Mack” Bishop**(Bass, Vocals) – The groove master, blending funk, jazz, and progressive rock into fluid, deep-pocket basslines.
🥁 **Eli “Fox” Foster **(Drums, Vocals) – The wild heartbeat, pushing the band forward with thunderous fills, polyrhythmic grooves, and explosive energy.

### **What Defines 4Castle?**

- **Multi-Layered Vocal Harmonies**– All five members **sing**, creating **rich, choral-like textures**that elevate every song.

- **Complex Arrangements & Bold Songwriting**– Each track is an **immersive journey**, balancing **technical mastery with raw emotion**.

- **Rhythmic Power & Innovation**– From **tight funk grooves to shifting time signatures**, their music **moves you-literally and figuratively**.

- **Lyrically Challenging Yet Accessible**– Their songs explore **deep themes of life, change, and resilience**, wrapped in **memorable hooks and melodies**.

### **4Castle’s Sound**

Imagine putting Emerson, Lake & Palmer, Heart, Led Zeppelin, Sly Stone, and Carlos Santana in a blender. The result? **4Castle**. Their music pays homage to the greats while pushing rock’s boundaries into the future.

### **Join the Journey**

With Maricopa Records, **4Castle **is set to redefine what a modern rock band can be. Expect genre-bending albums, electrifying performances, and music that resonates across generations.

🚀 Follow **4Castle **as they build their legacy-one song, one harmony, and one unforgettable moment at a time.

[](https://open.spotify.com/artist/4Cmxf7xuTHzf7UahMK2xJh)[](https://music.apple.com/us/artist/4castle/1801052673)![](https://www.maricoparecords.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/5-rooks-in-a-dark-setting-1.png)
## Releases

impression.text: The track opens with an intimate, melancholic atmosphere, featuring a sparse piano and a raw, narrative-driven vocal that establishes a deeply somber and reflective mood. It beautifully builds into a grand, theatrical ballad, driven by rich vocal harmonies, sweeping instrumentation, and a steady, dramatic rhythm that elevates the energy to an operatic, tragic climax. The production quality is highly polished, expertly balancing the space between dry, close-up verses and a dense, lushly reverberant chorus that fills the stereo field. A particularly striking feature is the sudden transition into the multi-layered vocal chorus, which acts as a powerful emotional release that defines the song's cinematic storytelling.

review.review_text: This is 4Castle at their most patient. The song opens nearly bare, a spoken-feeling vocal and sparse piano introducing Bentley, the lifer who watched 'the floor was poured' and stayed thirty-six years, and it earns its slow burn into that big harmonized chorus around the minute mark. The hook does real work: 'They took their cut. We bore the cost' lands with the plainspoken weight of a Springsteen factory song, and the C# major brightness underneath the bitterness gives the whole thing a stubborn dignity rather than just gloom. The final turn, 'So I sign off. I drop the key,' is the kind of small gesture that says more than a guitar solo would, and the band is smart enough to let it. A highlight, and one of their most affecting story-songs.
anchors_used: They took their cut. We bore the cost
So I sign off. I drop the key
sparse piano open building to harmonized chorus
